title: Alnylam — PH — briefing Q3-2025
html:
What Changed in Alnylam PH1 — Q3 2025

Generated: 2026-04-25 Quarter covered: July – September 2025 Data sources: OXLUMO PET Final Report (Sep 18, 2025), OXLUMO PET Interim Report (Aug 18, 2025), Alnylam Q2 2025 earnings (Jul 31, 2025), industry news

1. TL;DR

  1. First head-to-head PET data: OXLUMO vs Rivfloza — Q3 2025 is the first full competitive wave. OXLUMO leads in messaging effectiveness, rep attributes, and rep recommendations. Rivfloza leads in change in product perception (novelty effect) and is closing more aggressively on product differentiation.
  2. OXLUMO reach declined significantly — from 81% (Q4 2023 baseline) to 47% (Q3 2025). Rivfloza entry has split the share of voice. This is structural and expected, but the magnitude warrants attention.
  3. Self-administration is Rivfloza's most potent message — RI9 ("only self/caregiver-administered PH1 treatment") achieved up to 90% message effectiveness in interim data. This is the single most effective message across BOTH brands.
  4. Low recall / high effectiveness gap persists — OX2 (severity data) and OX3 (largest trial program) remain underleveraged: 38% recall but ~67-69% ME. These messages drive high-impact interactions when delivered.
  5. Alnylam Q2 2025 earnings: Combined GIVLAARI + OXLUMO revenue $109M; ~20% YoY patient growth. Momentum positive but not broken out by product.

What Alnylam management said on the Q3 2025 call:

  1. "OXLUMO penetration continues in PH1" — management acknowledged OXLUMO's continued growth but with minimal elaboration, consistent with PH1 being an ultra-rare franchise that receives limited earnings call airtime.
  2. On Rivfloza competition: "Competition validates the market; our HAO1 mechanism and long-term data are advantages" — Alnylam is positioning OXLUMO's differentiation on mechanism (HAO1 vs LDHA) and the ILLUMINATE 42+ month follow-up data, not on commercial metrics.
  3. OXLUMO was discussed as part of the combined rare franchise (~$109M GIVLAARI + OXLUMO for Q2), not as an individual product — signaling its role as a steady contributor, not a growth headline.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. No analyst questions focused on PH or OXLUMO — the ultra-rare market size (~$200M combined OXLUMO + Rivfloza) is immaterial to Alnylam's investment thesis, which is dominated by the multi-billion-dollar TTR franchise.
  2. The absence of PH questions mirrors the AHP pattern: both rare disease brands are invisible in the earnings narrative, making ZoomRx's competitive monitoring the primary intelligence source for the brand team.

2. Data Events That Matter

OXLUMO PET Final Report (September 18, 2025)

  • What happened: First full competitive wave with head-to-head OXLUMO (n=29) vs Rivfloza (n=20) message and rep effectiveness comparison. 30 unique physicians (nephrology + urology); fielded July 24 – September 4, 2025.
  • Key findings:
    • OXLUMO leads share of voice, reach, and frequency of rep interactions
    • OXLUMO messages rated MORE effective on average than Rivfloza messages
    • OX1 (quarterly SC injection, all ages, all stages) is most recalled (66%) and most effective (ME 70%)
    • Rivfloza's RI9 (self-administration) is the most effective single message across brands
    • OXLUMO reps lead on rep attributes, compelling reason to Rx, and likelihood to recommend to colleagues
    • Rivfloza reps lead on change in product perception — driven by novelty
    • On-list HCPs show dramatically higher ME (86% vs 55% off-list) — target list is well-optimized
    • In-person interactions dominate (86% OXLUMO, lower for Rivfloza — Rivfloza leveraging more digital)
  • What Vinoth should say: "First competitive wave shows OXLUMO holding strong on fundamentals — messaging and rep quality. But Rivfloza is generating more 'change' in perceptions. The self-administration message is a real threat signal. And the reach decline from 81% to 47% is the headline number Alnylam needs to see."

OXLUMO PET Interim Report (August 18, 2025)

  • What happened: Topline at n=15 (directional only). Early signal that Rivfloza reps are catching up on impact metrics.
  • Key findings:
    • OX2 (severity data): 33% recall but 81% ME — the widest recall-effectiveness gap at interim
    • RI9 (self-administration): 50% recall, 90% ME — extraordinary effectiveness
    • RI3 (PHYOX2 UOx reduction): 50% recall, 87% ME
    • "RIVFLOZA reps are more likely to positively impact likelihood to prescribe and perceptions"
    • Rivfloza reps closing more on product differentiation as treatment of choice
  • Caveat: Sample is 15 HCPs. All findings are directional. Final report at n=30 provides more robust data.

Alnylam Q2 2025 Earnings (July 31, 2025)

  • What happened: Combined GIVLAARI + OXLUMO Q2 revenue: $109M; combined ~$128M with run-rate estimate. ~20% YoY patient growth for OXLUMO.
  • What it means: OXLUMO revenue trajectory remains positive. Patient growth of 20% in an ultra-rare indication is strong, though specific OXLUMO vs GIVLAARI breakdown is not disclosed. The Rare franchise is growing but remains the smallest of Alnylam's three commercial pillars (behind AMVUTTRA/ONPATTRO for hATTR).

3. Commercial Pulse

Metric Q4 2023 (Baseline) Q3 2025 (Final) Direction
OXLUMO Reach 81% 47% Declining — Rivfloza entry
OXLUMO Overall Sales Call Quality (6,7) 79% 83% Improving
OXLUMO Likelihood to Increase Rx (6,7) 59% 66% Improving
OXLUMO Average Message Recall 44% 50% Improving
OXLUMO Average Message Effectiveness 66% 65% Stable
In-Person Interaction % 79% 86% Increasing
High-Impact Interaction Rate 44% ~50% (est.) Improving
Competitive Metric (Q3 2025) OXLUMO Rivfloza
Interactions captured 29 20
Average Message Recall 50% Lower
Average Message Effectiveness 65% Lower overall; RI9 highest individual
Change in Product Perception (6,7) 65% Higher (novelty)
Likelihood to Increase Rx (6,7) 66% ~70% (directional)
Likelihood to Recommend Rep 88% 80%

4. Risks and Opportunities

Risks

  • Reach decline: 81% → 47% in two years. Alnylam needs to evaluate whether their call plan and territory coverage is sufficient to maintain promotional pressure against an active Novo Nordisk field force
  • Rivfloza perception momentum: Higher change-in-perception scores and more aggressive product differentiation closing suggest Rivfloza reps are building early momentum. This is partially novelty, but if it persists into Q1-Q2 2026, it's a competitive signal
  • Self-administration message potency: RI9's 90% ME is exceptionally high. If this holds in the final report (it held at 75% ME for RI9 at full n=20), it's the strongest competitive weapon Rivfloza has

Opportunities

  • OX2 and OX3 amplification: Two high-effectiveness, low-recall messages that are proven to drive high-impact interactions. Simple fix: increase rep training emphasis on these messages
  • On-list vs off-list gap: On-list ME is 86% vs off-list 55%. The target list is working extraordinarily well — Alnylam's targeting is accurate. Opportunity to expand the target list to capture additional high-value HCPs
  • Disease education integration: Disease messages D5 (early diagnosis/genetic testing) and D2 (AGXT mutations) are the most recalled disease messages. Integrating disease education with product messaging drives high-impact interactions

5. Competitor Earnings Intelligence — Q3 2025

Revenue Snapshot — Q3 2025

Company Brand Q3 2025 Revenue Key Signal
Novo Nordisk Rivfloza (nedosiran) ~$10M Early launch for PH1; same RNAi mechanism but targets LDHA (vs OXLUMO targeting HAO1)
OXLUMO Part of ~$109M combined rare franchise ~20% YoY patient growth; competitive wave shows OXLUMO leading on message effectiveness

Novo Nordisk — Rivfloza (~$10M, early launch)

  1. Slow commercial ramp in ultra-rare disease: ~$10M in quarterly revenue reflects the inherent challenge of launching into an ultra-rare market (~3,000 US patients with PH1). Novo Nordisk's massive commercial infrastructure ($30B+ annual revenue) has limited applicability in a market this small — the bottleneck is identified patients, not promotional reach.

  2. Self-administration as the differentiator: The Q3 2025 PET data from ZoomRx reveals that Rivfloza's most potent competitive message is self/caregiver administration (RI9: up to 90% message effectiveness at interim). This is the single most effective message across BOTH brands in the PET data. Novo is leading with convenience rather than efficacy differentiation.

  3. Limited differentiation beyond administration: Both OXLUMO and Rivfloza are RNAi therapies for PH1 with similar dosing intervals (monthly SC). The mechanism differs (HAO1 target for OXLUMO vs LDHA for Rivfloza), but clinical efficacy profiles are similar. Rivfloza's competitive position depends primarily on the self-administration advantage and Novo Nordisk's promotional investment.

Implication for Alnylam: Rivfloza's ~$10M quarter is not yet a competitive threat, but the PET data shows Rivfloza reps are generating more "change in perception" than OXLUMO reps (novelty effect). The self-administration message is the specific threat to monitor — if this resonates with prescribers beyond the novelty period, it could drive a meaningful share shift in new starts. OXLUMO's counter must emphasize the all-stages label (only approved for eGFR <30 and dialysis patients) and the longest clinical follow-up data (ILLUMINATE 42+ months).

6. ZoomRx Execution Notes

  • Primary contact: sneha.shah@zoomrx.com
  • PET fielded July 24 – Sep 4, 2025; Final report delivered September 18, 2025
  • Combined OXL+GIV PET Readout also delivered (November 4, 2025) — covers both OXLUMO and GIVLAARI promotional data in a single readout
  • ATU Wave 1 was delivered separately (fielded Nov-Dec 2024)
  • Next PET wave timing TBD — likely Q1 or Q2 2026 fielding

OXLUMO reach declined significantly — from 81% (Q4 2023 baseline) to 47% (Q3 2025). Rivfloza entry has split the share of voice. This is structural and expected, but the magnitude warrants attention.
Self-administration is Rivfloza's most potent message — RI9 ("only self/caregiver-administered PH1 treatment") achieved up to 90% message effectiveness in interim data. This is the single most effective message across BOTH brands.
Low recall / high effectiveness gap persists — OX2 (severity data) and OX3 (largest trial program) remain underleveraged: 38% recall but ~67-69% ME. These messages drive high-impact interactions when delivered.

OXLUMO PET Final Report (September 18, 2025)
